## /visible-par-les-ia

**Titre :** Votre site est-il visible par les IA ? · Test gratuit · Mathieu Haye
**Description :** Testez en 30 secondes si ChatGPT, Perplexity et les IA peuvent lire ET citer votre site. Un score, un verdict clair, et ce que vous pouvez gagner. Gratuit.

Test gratuit · 30 secondes

# Votre site est-il visible par les IA ?

ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI : vos clients posent leurs questions à des IA. Si votre site n’est pas lisible et citable, vous êtes invisible. Testez-le maintenant.

## Analysez votre site

On lit votre page comme une IA le ferait : accès des robots, structure, données, fichiers de citation. Résultat immédiat, sans engagement.
L’URL de votre site
Votre site tourne sur WordPress 100% code / sur-mesure Shopify Wix Webflow Framer Autre / je ne sais pas
Analyser mon site →
Connexion au site…
0 / 100
…

## …

…
Visible0%
Citable0%
Agent-ready0%
Ce que voient les IA
Ce que vous pouvez gagner
… → …
Votre score est prêt. Entrez votre email ci-dessous pour le révéler.

### Recevez le rapport complet

Je vous envoie le détail signal par signal + ce qu’il faut corriger en priorité. Et si vous voulez, je m’en occupe pour vous.
Tester un autre site
La visibilité IA, c'est la capacité des intelligences artificielles (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude) à lire votre site ET à le citer quand vos clients leur posent une question. Le test ci-dessus la mesure sur 100 en 30 secondes, sans inscription : il analyse votre page exactement comme un robot d'IA le ferait.
L'essentiel en 30 secondes
Près de la moitié des sites de PME sont illisibles pour les IA : contenu chargé en JavaScript, robots bloqués, zéro donnée structurée.
Le GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) est au moteur de réponse ce que le SEO était à Google : il décide si une IA vous cite, ou cite votre concurrent.
Trois niveaux comptent : être visible (l'IA accède au contenu), citable (elle peut vous attribuer une réponse), prêt pour les agents (les assistants IA peuvent agir sur votre site).
Le test est gratuit, le score est immédiat, et il vous dit précisément ce qui plafonne votre site.

## Qu'est-ce que la visibilité IA, et pourquoi ça change tout ?

La visibilité IA mesure si un moteur de réponse génératif peut trouver, lire et réutiliser le contenu de votre site. Quand un client demande « quel prestataire pour X ? » à ChatGPT ou Perplexity, l'IA répond en citant quelques sources ; si votre site n'est pas lisible par elle, vous n'existez tout simplement pas dans cette réponse. En 2026, cette couche IA s'intercale de plus en plus entre vos clients et vous, avant même le premier clic sur Google.

## Comment savoir si ChatGPT peut lire votre site ?

Lancez le test en haut de cette page : il récupère votre page d'accueil comme le ferait un robot d'IA et vérifie en direct ce qu'elle peut en extraire. Il contrôle trois choses : l'accès (vos fichiers robots.txt et votre HTML laissent-ils passer les IA ?), la lisibilité (le contenu est-il dans le HTML, ou caché derrière du JavaScript ?) et la structure de citation (données structurées, fichiers dédiés aux IA comme llms.txt). Le résultat est un score sur 100 et un verdict en une phrase.

## SEO ou GEO : quelle différence ?

Le SEO vous place dans les liens bleus de Google ; le GEO vous place dans la réponse générée par l'IA. Le premier optimise pour un classement de pages, le second pour une citation dans un texte. Les deux partagent une base technique (HTTPS, contenu lisible, données structurées), mais le GEO ajoute une couche que la plupart des prestataires SEO ignorent encore : les fichiers et formats que les IA préfèrent, comme llms.txt, les versions Markdown des pages et les données structurées enrichies.
Vous croiserez plusieurs sigles pour la même chose : visibilité IA, GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), GSO (Generative Search Optimization) et AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) décrivent tous le même objectif, être cité par les IA. Le jargon change, pas la discipline. Quel que soit le sigle, la seule question qui compte est de savoir si l'IA vous recommande, vous ou un concurrent. Ce test et mon accompagnement les couvrent tous.

## Pourquoi les IA ne citent pas encore votre site

Trois raisons reviennent presque toujours. Un : le contenu est rendu en JavaScript et le robot ne voit qu'une coquille vide. Deux : le site n'a aucune donnée structurée, donc l'IA voit du texte mais ne sait pas qui dit quoi. Trois : il manque les signaux dédiés aux IA (llms.txt, en-têtes de préférence, version Markdown), si bien que le site est « vu mais ignoré ». Le test vous dit lequel de ces trous vous concerne.

## Pourquoi corriger sa visibilité IA n'est pas un bricolage

Ce n'est jamais un réglage unique. Plusieurs couches techniques se superposent, des accès des robots jusqu'aux signaux que les IA préfèrent, et elles n'ont pas le même poids selon votre plateforme. Le terrain bouge vite : ce qui comptait il y a six mois a déjà changé, et il suffit d'un détail mal posé pour rester invisible alors que tout le reste est en place. C'est précisément pour ça que la plupart des entreprises délèguent : pas parce que c'est sorcier, mais parce que c'est mouvant, facile à rater, et que chaque point manqué se paie en citations perdues au profit d'un concurrent. Lancez le test en haut de page pour voir où vous plafonnez, puis voyez comment on travaille ensemble pour que ce soit corrigé pour de bon.

## Questions fréquentes

### Le test de visibilité IA est-il vraiment gratuit ?

Oui. Vous entrez l'adresse de votre site et vous obtenez un score sur 100 et un verdict immédiat, sans inscription. Le rapport détaillé signal par signal vous est envoyé par email si vous le demandez.

### Quelle différence entre la visibilité IA et le référencement Google classique ?

Le référencement Google vise un classement dans les résultats de recherche ; la visibilité IA vise une citation dans la réponse d'un assistant comme ChatGPT, Perplexity ou Google AI Overviews. Un site bien référencé sur Google peut rester invisible pour les IA.

### GEO, GSO, AEO : c'est la même chose ?

Oui, ce sont des quasi-synonymes de la même discipline : être visible et cité par les IA. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), GSO (Generative Search Optimization) et AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) décrivent le même objectif sous des sigles différents. Ce test et le service les couvrent tous.

### Les IA peuvent-elles lire un site WordPress, Shopify, Wix, Webflow ou Framer ?

WordPress et les sites sur-mesure atteignent les trois niveaux : visible, citable, prêt pour les agents. Shopify, Wix, Webflow et Framer permettent l'essentiel du on-page mais bloquent les fichiers servis à la racine et les agents IA ; le levier devient alors une migration vers un CMS ouvert ou un développement sur-mesure.

### Combien de temps pour améliorer son score de visibilité IA ?

Sur un site ouvert, l'essentiel se gagne vite, souvent en quelques jours. Le test affiche directement le score probable que vous pouvez atteindre après corrections : c'est la mesure de l'écart entre là où vous êtes et là où vous pourriez être. Plutôt que d'y passer vos soirées, vous me confiez les corrections et vous récupérez un site que les IA citent.

### Qui est derrière ce test de visibilité IA ?

Mathieu Haye, freelance builder à Paris spécialisé en visibilité IA, GEO et développement sur-mesure. Le test est gratuit et sert à diagnostiquer ; si vous voulez que les corrections soient faites pour vous, on en parle.
Vous savez maintenant où vous en êtes. Pour passer de « invisible » à « cité par les IA », voyez comment on travaille ensemble, automatisez tout avec Biencite, mon outil de visibilité IA qui génère et déploie les correctifs et suit vos citations, posez vos questions à mon agent IA, ou explorez le journal sur l'IA appliquée et le GEO.
À explorer ensuite Test maturité IA Développeur d'agents IA Journal Discuter avec mon IA

## /en/ai-agent-developer

**Titre :** Freelance AI agent developer · Immediate answers for your customers · Mathieu Haye
**Description :** Freelance AI agent developer: your customers get an immediate answer, your team keeps the cases that matter. Custom agents, from scoping to production.

AI agent development

# Immediate answers for your customers, from a freelance AI agent developer.

Your team keeps the cases that matter: an agent handles the recurring questions, the sorting of inbound requests, the search through your documents. I design it, connect it to your tools and put it in production. If a simple form or an FAQ is enough, I will tell you. An AI agent has to earn its place.
What an agent does for you

## An agent is a slow task that disappears.

Every agent targets a measurable outcome: time handed back to your teams, a faster answer for your customers, a lead handled even outside office hours.

## 24/7 customer answers

A conversational agent that answers your customers' questions instantly, at any hour, instead of a 20-minute search through your docs or an email that waits until tomorrow.
Answers in seconds, not in 20 minutes
Draws on your real documents
Cites sources, says when it does not know
Hands off to a human on sensitive cases
Best for
customer support, product FAQ, internal help, cutting the volume of repetitive tickets.
Discuss this case
Most requested

## Leads qualified on arrival

An agent that sorts and qualifies your inbound requests before your sales team touches them: it asks the right questions, scores the lead, fills the CRM and flags the priorities.
Qualifies 24/7, even outside office hours
Writes straight into your CRM
Surfaces hot leads first
Your reps only talk to the right contacts
Best for
B2B prospecting, inbound forms, sales teams wasting time on cold leads.
Discuss this case

## A first draft in seconds

A copilot that searches your knowledge base and drafts a first version from your own documents: a summary, a template reply, a case digest.
Synthesises several documents at once
Drafts something to validate, not to retype
Stays in your tone and vocabulary
Saves hours on repetitive tasks
Best for
support, legal, HR, operations: any team that searches and drafts from internal documents.
Discuss this case
Your use case is not on the list? That is normal: an agent is built around one precise task. We frame yours in 30 min.
How a project runs

## From scoping to production, no detour.

We target one task, check the agent does it well, then push it to production and expand it. You see a result before investing more.
01

### Use-case scoping

We start from the expected outcome, not the tech. Which slow task to remove, on what data, with what measurable gain. 30 min is enough to decide.
02

### Testable prototype

A first focused agent, wired to a sample of your real data. We test it on real questions and adjust before going further.
03

### Production rollout

We connect the agent to your tools, add the guardrails (human validation, logs, limits), and deploy it where your teams already work.
04

### Follow-up and expansion

We watch real usage, fix the blind spots, open new cases. Through a monthly support plan, on quote, if you want a regular presence.
Framework & trust

## Serious about the code and your data.

An AI agent touches your data and speaks in your name. The framework matters as much as the build. Here is how I work.
01

### You own the code

A Git repository you own, admin access, clear documentation. No hidden lock-in: everything is transferable to another team whenever you want.
02

### GDPR & EU hosting

Hosting available in France or Europe. We restrict what is sent to the models and isolate sensitive data.
03

### One point of contact

The person who scopes is the one who codes, deploys and maintains. No hidden subcontracting, no middleman between you and the code. You have my number.
04

### Maintenance & NDA

Maintenance through a monthly support plan, on quote, at your pace. NDA signed on request before any detailed discussion about your product or data.
FAQ

## Questions I get about agents.

Is a custom AI agent reliable in production?
Yes, provided it is scoped correctly. A reliable agent is bounded: it answers within a defined perimeter, cites its sources when it draws on your documents, and knows how to say it does not know rather than inventing. Guardrails are added (human validation on sensitive cases, readable logs, tests on real questions) before going to production. Reliability comes from the scoping, not from a model left loose.
What happens to my data?
Your data stays yours. The agent is only wired to the sources you authorise. An NDA can be signed before any detailed discussion.
How long does it take to build an AI agent?
A first useful agent is often delivered in a few weeks. We start with a prototype focused on one precise use case (answering a typical question, qualifying a lead), quickly testable, then push it to production and expand it. A simple conversational agent ships in one to two weeks; a copilot wired to a document base takes a bit more.
How much does a custom AI agent cost?
The price depends on scope, framed in 30 minutes. A first scope starts as a Sprint from €400; a full agent or a copilot is priced on quote, depending on the sources to connect. Add the usage cost of the models, pay as you go, and a monthly support plan on quote if you want ongoing help.
Can the agent plug into my tools?
Yes. A useful agent is connected: it reads and writes in your CRM, your document base, your inbox or your business tools. It can trigger actions (create a record, send an email, notify a team), not just answer, within a perimeter validated during scoping.
How is this different from a regular chatbot?
A regular chatbot follows pre-written scripts and stalls the moment you step off the path. A custom AI agent understands the request in natural language, retrieves the answer from your real data, and can act in your tools. It does not recite a decision tree: it reasons within a scoped perimeter, cites its sources, and hands off to a human when needed.

## Shall we scope your agent?

Describe the task you want to hand to an agent. In 30 min, I tell you if it is doable, how, and how long it takes. No commitment.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email
Mathieu Haye is a freelance AI agent developer based in Paris. He builds custom agents that answer your customers 24/7, qualify your inbound requests and retrieve an answer from your documents in seconds. The starting point is always the same: a slow manual task to remove, with a measurable gain attached.

## AI agent developer or agency: which to choose?

With a freelance developer, you talk directly to the person building the agent. Decisions are made fast, without the structural overhead of an agency. For a focused agent, an internal copilot or an automation, it is a fast and economical route. For a product that needs several teams in parallel over months, an agency remains the better fit.

## A few delivered projects

The examples are anonymised. One client replaced their off-the-shelf CRM with a fully custom CRM, built around their processes. A weekly AI newsletter goes out every week with no manual work. A B2B prospecting pipeline and several automations run in production.

## The technical side, in short

Document search over your own data (RAG), readable orchestration with n8n, API connections to your tools: the setup stays simple and transferable.
You can see what an agent can do for you. To go further, book a scoping call or describe your need to my AI agent. See also: the AI agent for SMEs, n8n automation, the AI freelance, a custom CRM, a custom web app, AI visibility (GEO), your AI maturity, the home page and the journal.

How to start

## One process, one week, one result.

Rather than transforming everything at once, we target one specific pain point. A useful agent ships, the time saved is measured, and you decide what comes next.
01

### We pick ONE process

In 30 minutes, we spot the heaviest repetitive task for your team. Just one to start.
02

### A Sprint from 400 EUR

A first agent, scoped, built and shipped. You test without a heavy commitment.
03

### We measure time saved

You put it in your team's hands right away. The benefit shows from the first days of real use.
04

### We extend if it's worth it

A second process, an integration with your CRM, ongoing monthly support on a quoted basis. You decide, at your own pace.
Trust & data

## Your data stays with you.

Concrete answers about data security: what I commit to, and what I never do.
01

### GDPR & EU hosting

Your data stays in Europe, limited to the strict minimum. Nothing is resold, nothing is reused for other purposes.
02

### The agent is yours

Code, admin access, clear documentation. Transferable whenever you want, with no proprietary lock-in.
03

### One human contact

You talk to the person who scopes, builds and trains your team. NDA signed on request.
04

### Calm maintenance

Evolutions and support via a monthly plan, quoted to your needs. You're never left alone with the tool once it's delivered.
FAQ

## The questions an SME asks.

Isn't AI too complex for my small business?
No. You need neither an IT department nor in-house technical skills. You describe the need in plain business terms, I handle the technical side, and you get a tool that is simple to use day to day. We start with a single process so adoption stays straightforward.
Isn't it too expensive for a small company?
A first agent on one focused process starts from 400 EUR with the one-week Sprint format. We measure the time saved, then extend only if the results justify it.
What happens to my data and my customers' data?
It stays yours. The agent only accesses the data it strictly needs, what is processed is documented, and nothing is resold or reused for other purposes. You keep full control.
Will an AI agent replace my staff?
No. The agent takes on the repetitive tasks: answering common questions, sorting, qualifying, drafting a first version. Your team keeps what needs human judgment: relationships, complex cases, decisions.
How long before I see results?
On a well-chosen first process, a useful agent ships in about a week. The time saved shows from the first days of real use, and we adjust based on field feedback.
Who owns the agent once it's delivered?
Yours. Everything is transferable if you want to hand maintenance to someone else tomorrow. Evolutions can run through a monthly support plan, at your own pace.

## Got a process in mind? Let's talk.

Describe the task that takes the most of your team's time. I'll reply with an opinion and a quote, with no commitment.

Trust

## A clear frame, end to end.

Here is what applies to every project, whatever its size. Nothing more, nothing less.
01

### You own the code

A Git repository you own, admin access, clear documentation. Transferable to another team with no hidden dependencies, at any time.
02

Projects designed with your obligations in mind: data hosted in Europe when it requires it, AI processing scoped to limit exposure of sensitive information.

FAQ

## Questions before starting.

Why a solo AI freelance rather than a team?
You talk directly to the person who designs and writes the code, no project manager, no subcontracting. Decisions take one conversation. For a very large product run by several teams in parallel, an agency remains the better fit, and I will say so.
How fast is it delivered?
A simple AI agent or a focused automation: one week. A full application with built-in AI: 4 to 6 weeks, quoted per project, with a usable first version in production then iterations after real-world use. The exact timeline is set at scoping.
Do you work remotely and in English?
Yes. Scoping, demos and documentation happen over video, in French or English, with code and documentation available online.
Do you work with startups as well as SMEs?
Yes. The format adapts: a focused automation or AI agent for an SME, a full MVP for a startup. In both cases, a first version reaches production quickly, then evolves through iterations.
What if my need does not justify custom development?
I tell you at scoping. Sometimes an existing tool, properly configured, is enough; in that case I point you to it. Sometimes the right answer is to build nothing.

## Talk about your AI project?

Describe your need in 30 seconds. I will tell you if I can do it, how long it takes and in which format, with no commitment.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email
Mathieu Haye is a freelance developer specialized in AI and automation, based in France. He designs and ships custom AI agents, automations and business applications, from scoping to production, in a matter of weeks. The same person scopes, develops, deploys and maintains: decisions are fast and nothing gets lost between the need and the delivery.

## What results can you expect from a freelance AI expert?

Time given back to your teams, fewer entry errors, information flowing without copy-paste. Two public examples: a custom CRM that replaced an off-the-shelf CRM that no longer fit, so the team now works in a tool that follows its processes instead of constraining them; and an AI newsletter produced automatically end to end, where each issue used to require manual work. The other services follow the same logic: custom AI agents, n8n automation, custom web apps & MVPs, AI agent for SMEs and AI visibility (GEO).

## AI developer in Paris or remote?

Based in the Paris region, I work on-site in the Ile-de-France area as well as remotely, internationally included. Scoping happens over video, code and documentation are available online, and exchanges fit into a short weekly check-in. Meetings can be held in English.

## How do you get started with a freelance in artificial intelligence?

It all starts with a 30-minute scoping call over video, free and with no commitment. No written spec needed: you explain the outcome you are aiming for, in plain business terms, and you leave with a recommended format and a timeline estimate. If the project is not ready, or if an existing tool is enough, I say so. On the technical side: n8n for orchestration, Next.js and Supabase for applications, the leading AI model APIs for agents.

AI maturity is the degree to which your company actually uses artificial intelligence and automation in its daily work, from centralised data all the way to AI agents in production. The free diagnostic at the top of this page measures it in under a minute: it places you on a level, gives you a score and lists your priority levers, with no sign-up.
The essentials in 30 seconds
AI maturity reads across three levels: to activate (everything is manual), in motion (the first automations are running), mature (AI agents and orchestration in place).
The right starting point is not the technology but the use case that pays off most for you: aiming right separates an immediate gain from a project that stalls.
For the least-equipped profiles, the first gains are measured in hours per week within the first month.
The diagnostic is free, the score is instant, and it tells you exactly where your time is being lost.

## What is a company's AI maturity?

AI maturity measures how far a company has built artificial intelligence and automation into its real operations, not just its intentions. A low-maturity small business runs on Excel and manual work; a mature one runs AI agents and workflows in production. Most companies sit between the two, having started without structuring anything. The diagnostic at the top of the page turns that gut feeling into a clear score, based on your tools, your data, your processes and your current use of AI.

## How do I assess my company's AI maturity?

The simplest way is not to do it blind. The diagnostic at the top of this page places you in under a minute, where a gut-feel self-assessment would mostly tell you what you want to hear. You get a score, a level and your priority levers, with no sign-up. What really counts comes next: the score tells you where you stand, not what to do with your specific situation. That is the whole point of working together, turning that snapshot into automations and AI agents running in your business, instead of one more report that sits in a folder.

## The real cost of staying on Excel and manual work

Doing nothing is not neutral: every week, hours go into double data entry, into data hunted across ten files and into forgotten follow-ups, while better-equipped competitors answer faster and hold up at scale. The trap, when you try to do it alone, is spreading yourself across ten projects and finishing none, or starting on the wrong one. That is why it begins with a diagnostic that says where the return is, then a partnership that puts the right levers into production, in the right order. Not a tutorial to follow at night: a partner who builds while you run your business. Take the test above to size up the stakes, then see how we work together.

## What are the levels of AI maturity?

There are three levels, and the test assigns you yours. To activate: lots of Excel and manual work, AI not yet in place, potential everywhere and gains that come fast. In motion: the foundations are there, a few automations are running, what remains is connecting the tools, hardening what is fragile and embedding AI into key processes. Mature: AI agents and advanced orchestration in production, where the game becomes targeted optimisation and custom internal tools. Each level comes with its own diagnostic and tailored recommendations.

## How much time can AI save you?

For the least-equipped profiles, the first gains are measured in hours per week within the first month: one AI agent, a few workflows and a clean CRM are often enough to remove repetitive tasks. As maturity rises, the gains shift toward reliability and scale, automations that hold up in production and business agents on your critical workflows. The diagnostic estimates where your time is being lost and quantifies the levers to act on first, rather than offering a generic promise.

## Frequently asked questions

### Is the AI maturity diagnostic really free?

Yes. You pick the quick version (6 questions) or the complete one (20 questions) and you immediately get a score, a level and your priority levers, with no sign-up. The detailed analysis of your answers, with 2-3 concrete quick wins, is sent to you by email if you ask for it.

### What is the difference between AI maturity and digital maturity?

Digital maturity covers your whole stack and your digital habits; AI maturity focuses on what artificial intelligence and automation concretely change in your work. A company can be well equipped on the SaaS side yet immature on AI, because its processes stay manual and its data goes unused.

### Do you need a tech team to get started with AI?

No. Many small businesses start with nobody in-house: that is exactly the role of a freelance builder, to scope, build and ship the first automations and the first AI agent. The diagnostic takes your current technical capacity into account so it proposes realistic levers, not ones out of reach.

### Which use case gives a small business the best return on AI?

It depends entirely on where you lose time today, and it is rarely where you think. The right starting point is not a checklist: it is the one that, for you, has the best return for the least effort. The diagnostic at the top of the page exists precisely to spot it, and the partnership to put it into production. Aiming right the first time is what separates an immediate gain from a project that stalls.

### Who is behind this AI maturity diagnostic?

Mathieu Haye, a freelance builder in Paris specialised in CRM, data and applied AI. The diagnostic is free and serves to place you; if you want the automations and AI agents built for you, let's talk.
You now know where you stand. To move from diagnostic to concrete automations, see how we work together, ask my AI agent your questions, or also test whether your site is visible to AI.

Test another site
AI visibility is whether AI assistants (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude) can read your site AND cite it when your customers ask them a question. The test above measures it out of 100 in 30 seconds, no signup: it analyses your page exactly like an AI crawler would.
The gist in 30 seconds
Nearly half of SME sites are unreadable to AI: content loaded in JavaScript, crawlers blocked, zero structured data.
GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is to answer engines what SEO was to Google: it decides whether an AI cites you, or cites your competitor.
Three layers matter: being visible (AI reaches the content), citable (it can attribute an answer to you), agent-ready (AI assistants can act on your site).
The test is free, the score is instant, and it tells you exactly what is capping your site.

## What is AI visibility, and why it changes everything

AI visibility measures whether a generative answer engine can find, read and reuse your site content. When a customer asks ChatGPT or Perplexity "which provider for X?", the AI answers by citing a few sources; if your site is not readable to it, you simply do not exist in that answer. In 2026, this AI layer increasingly sits between your customers and you, before the first click on Google.

## How do you know if ChatGPT can read your site?

Run the test at the top of this page: it fetches your homepage the way an AI crawler would and checks, live, what it can extract. It looks at three things: access (do your robots.txt and HTML let AI in?), readability (is the content in the HTML, or hidden behind JavaScript?) and citation structure (structured data, AI-dedicated files like llms.txt). The result is a score out of 100 and a one-line verdict.

## SEO vs GEO: what is the difference?

SEO places you in Google's blue links; GEO places you inside the AI-generated answer. The first optimises for a page ranking, the second for a citation inside a text. Both share a technical base (HTTPS, readable content, structured data), but GEO adds a layer most SEO providers still ignore: the files and formats AI prefers, like llms.txt, Markdown versions of pages, and enriched structured data.
You will run into several acronyms for the same thing: AI visibility, GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), GSO (Generative Search Optimization) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) all describe the same goal, being cited by AI. The jargon changes, the discipline does not. Whatever the acronym, the only question that matters is whether the AI recommends you or a competitor. This test and my service cover them all.

## Why AI does not cite your site yet

Three reasons come up almost every time. One: the content is rendered in JavaScript and the crawler only sees an empty shell. Two: the site has no structured data, so AI sees text but cannot tell who says what. Three: it lacks the AI-dedicated signals (llms.txt, preference headers, Markdown version), so the site is "seen but ignored". The test tells you which of these gaps applies to you.

## Why fixing AI visibility is not a DIY job

It is never a single setting. Several technical layers stack on top of each other, from crawler access to the signals AI prefers, and they do not carry the same weight depending on your platform. The ground moves fast: what mattered six months ago has already shifted, and one detail in the wrong place is enough to stay invisible while everything else is in order. That is exactly why most companies delegate it: not because it is rocket science, but because it is moving, easy to get wrong, and every missed point is paid for in citations lost to a competitor. Run the test at the top of the page to see where you are capped, then see how we work together to get it fixed for good.

## Frequently asked questions

### Is the AI visibility test really free?

Yes. Enter your site address and get a score out of 100 and an instant verdict, no signup. The full signal-by-signal report is emailed to you on request.

### What is the difference between AI visibility and classic Google SEO?

Google SEO targets a ranking in search results; AI visibility targets a citation inside an assistant answer, like ChatGPT, Perplexity or Google AI Overviews. A site that ranks well on Google can still be invisible to AI.

### GEO, GSO, AEO: are they the same thing?

Yes, they are near-synonyms for the same discipline: being visible and cited by AI. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), GSO (Generative Search Optimization) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) describe the same goal under different acronyms. This test and the service cover them all.

### Can AI read a WordPress, Shopify, Wix, Webflow or Framer site?

WordPress and custom-built sites reach all three layers: visible, citable, agent-ready. Shopify, Wix, Webflow and Framer allow most on-page work but block root-served files and AI agents; the lever then becomes migrating to an open CMS or a custom build.

### How long does it take to improve an AI visibility score?

On an open site, most of it is won fast, often within days. The test shows directly the score you can reach after fixes: the measure of the gap between where you are and where you could be. Rather than spending your evenings on it, you hand the fixes to me and get back a site AI cites.

### Who is behind this AI visibility test?

Mathieu Haye, a freelance builder in Paris specialising in AI visibility, GEO and custom development. The test is free and diagnostic; if you want the fixes done for you, let's talk.
Now you know where you stand. To go from "invisible" to "cited by AI", see how we work together, automate it all with Biencite, my AI-visibility tool that generates and deploys the fixes and tracks your citations, ask my AI agent, or browse the journal on applied AI and GEO.

How I decide

## First decide, then build.

Scoping ends in one of three outcomes: build, plug in an existing tool, or do nothing for now. I recommend the simplest solution that meets the need.
01

### A useful V1 first

We slice the scope. A first usable version in weeks, in your hands. We iterate after real-world use, not before.
02

### Fast, never sloppy

Judgement, review and testing stay human: nothing reaches production without validation.
03

### Your tools finally talk to each other

Reminders, notifications, invoicing, syncing between your software: repetitive tasks run on their own, in a readable, editable way.
04

### No middleman

I scope, I code, I deploy, I maintain. You have my phone number. No project manager filtering your requests.
FAQ

## Questions I get often.

What if I don’t know exactly what I need?
That is the most common situation. You do not need a written spec: just describe the outcome you are aiming for, in plain business terms. The 30-minute scoping call turns a fuzzy idea into a clear perimeter, and you leave with a clear recommendation, with no commitment. If the project is not ready, I say so and point you in the right direction.
Why not an agency?
An agency bills 3 to 5 times more for the same deliverable, because it has to fund salespeople, project managers and back-office. Here, you have a single point of contact: the person who scopes, codes and delivers. Decisions move faster. For a product that needs more than ten developers in parallel, an agency remains the right choice; for an MVP, an internal tool or an automation, it is not.
How do you guarantee code quality?
I rely on modern tools to write and document faster. I review, test and validate every delivery before it goes to production. You receive readable, documented code.
What tools do you work with?
Proven building blocks: Next.js, Supabase and Stripe for applications, n8n for automations, AI agents on the OpenAI or Claude APIs, and Claude Code as a development assistant.
Who owns the code, and where is the data hosted?
The code is yours: a Git repository in your name, admin access, clear documentation. Your data can be hosted in France, with GDPR compliance built in from the start. Everything is transferable to another team, at any time.
What if I already have a tech team?
Then I step in as targeted reinforcement (AI integration, CRM rebuild, automation), alongside your team. Collaboration runs through a shared workspace and a short weekly check-in.
Do we need to sign a contract before starting?
Yes. Every engagement starts with a written quote and, if you want, a signed non-disclosure agreement before any detailed discussion about your product. Nothing begins on a verbal handshake alone.
Do you work remotely and in English?
Yes. I am based in Paris and work remotely with clients across France and internationally. Exchanges happen over video, and English calls are fine from scoping through to demos.

## Talk now?

Describe your project in 30 seconds. I will tell you whether I can do it, and how long it takes, within 24 hours.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email

## /en/custom-crm

**Titre :** Custom CRM development · Freelance CRM consultant · Mathieu Haye
**Description :** Freelance CRM consultant: a CRM your team actually uses. Tuning of your current tool, automations, or a custom CRM you fully own, code included.

Custom CRM

# A custom CRM your team actually opens.

Your team deserves a CRM it actually opens. Depending on your situation, I tune the tool you already have, I connect your tools to each other, or I build the one that follows the way you work. If a well-configured HubSpot solves your problem, I will advise against a custom CRM.
Three delivery modes

## Three possible answers, only one right for you.

Custom is not always the right answer: sometimes a tune-up is enough. Scoping exists to pick the cheapest mode that actually solves the problem.

## Setup & repair of a market CRM

Pipedrive, HubSpot or Brevo configured around your actual process, or an existing CRM put back on its feet: a simpler pipeline, fields that matter, a trained team.
Pipedrive, HubSpot, Brevo configuration
Repair of an existing market CRM
Data migration, deduplication, access rights
Team training on its own pipeline
The right mode when
the tool is right but badly wired: confusing pipeline, empty fields, a team re-typing everything in Excel on the side.
Discuss this mode
The signature mode

## Fully custom CRM

When your business does not fit the boxes of a generic tool, the CRM gets modeled on your real processes. That is the path taken for e-Enfance / 3018: an all-in-one, sovereign CRM that replaced their market CRM.
Modeled on your real processes, not on a template
Multichannel and realtime where your work needs it
Sensitive data handled, hosted wherever you decide
No per-seat licence
The right mode when
your work is specific: associations, SMEs with atypical processes, sensitive data, teams growing without wanting to pay per seat.
Discuss this mode

## Integrations & automations around your CRM

Your CRM is fine, but everything around it is manual. I connect billing, support, email and enrichment with n8n: records create and complete themselves, and the right people get notified at the right time.
Automatic sync between your CRM and your other tools
Automatic record enrichment
Targeted Slack or email notifications
No more double data entry
The right mode when
the data already exists somewhere, and someone copies it into the CRM by hand every single day.
Discuss this mode
The right mode gets picked during a 30-minute scoping call. If tuning your current tool is enough, I say so and we stop there.
Real case · e-Enfance / 3018

## Market CRM first, custom when it had to be.

e-Enfance is the French child-protection association that operates 3018, the national helpline for young victims of online violence. Before, the helpline teams worked with a market CRM pushed to its limits and permanent workarounds. Today, they work in a single tool modeled on their real situations. I led that transition end to end, and the project is public.
01

### Worked on their market CRM

I first made the existing platform serve the helpline teams, with no anti-vendor dogma.
02

### Structural limits

Missing multichannel intake, helpline flows the tool could not model, highly sensitive data: every change was paid for in complexity.
03

### An informed exit

Replacement was only proposed after every possible tune-up had been tried. The new CRM was designed around how the 3018 teams actually work.
04

### A sovereign CRM in production

Multichannel, realtime, sensitive data protected. The teams work in a tool designed for them, and the association owns its system.
This reference is public and detailed on the projects page. Other CRM work stays anonymized.
Trust & compliance

## Your data, your code, your rules.

A CRM holds your clients, your deals, and sometimes far more sensitive material. The frame is set before the first line of code.
•

### You own the code

Git repository in your name, admin access, documentation. Bring it in-house or switch providers at any time, with no hidden dependency.
•

### GDPR and France / EU hosting

Data minimization, role-based access rights and a hosting location chosen to match your requirements, all designed in from the start.
•

### One single point of contact

The person who scopes is the person who codes, deploys and maintains. No subcontracting: you always know who touches your data.
•

### Maintenance and NDA

Ongoing care through a monthly retainer: fixes, evolutions, adaptations when a connected tool changes. NDA signed on request, before any detailed exchange.
FAQ

## Questions about custom CRM work.

Off-the-shelf CRM or custom CRM: how do I choose?
Always start with the market. If Pipedrive, HubSpot or Brevo covers most of your process, good configuration will cost less than development. Custom is justified when workarounds pile up: processes the tool cannot model, missing multichannel intake, sensitive data, per-seat licences inflating as the team grows. That is the diagnosis made at e-Enfance before replacing their market CRM.
How long does a custom CRM take?
Tuning or repair is counted in days. Automations, in days or weeks. A fully custom CRM is built over several weeks: first a V1 covering the most critical flow, put in the team's hands, then iterations. The precise timeline is set during the 30-minute scoping call.
What about my data, where is it hosted?
Wherever you decide. For a custom CRM, the servers are chosen with you and you hold the keys. That is the model used for e-Enfance / 3018, which handles particularly sensitive data. For a market tool, hosting depends on the vendor: that criterion enters the selection from the very first call.
Do I keep the code of the CRM?
Yes, entirely, with the documentation to go with it. You can bring maintenance in-house or hand it to another team at any time. The monthly retainer is an option, not an obligation.
Can you fix an existing market CRM?
Yes. A slow CRM, automations stacked on automations, unreadable fields, a team re-typing everything in Excel: that can be repaired without throwing everything away. And if the audit shows the tool is no longer the right one for you, I say so, with arguments.
How much does a custom CRM cost?
A short, tightly scoped first engagement, the Sprint, starts at €400. A fully custom CRM is quoted based on channels, processes and data requirements. The quote is fixed after the 30-minute scoping call.

## A CRM your team will actually use.

Tell me how your team works today, even if it all lives in Excel. In 30 minutes I tell you which of the three modes solves your problem, and whether a simple tune-up is enough.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email
Mathieu Haye is a freelance CRM consultant based in Paris. He works at three levels: setup and repair of market CRMs (Pipedrive, HubSpot, Brevo), automations around an existing CRM, and fully custom CRM development when market tools genuinely do not fit. Public reference: the CRM built for the French child-protection association e-Enfance / 3018, which replaced their market CRM.

## What does a freelance CRM consultant actually do?

A freelance CRM consultant covers what a classic CRM integrator does, without the agency structure around it. For an SME, that means: choosing the tool that matches the real process, configuring it, migrating and deduplicating the data, wiring the useful automations and training the team on its own pipeline. For an association or nonprofit, specific constraints come on top: volunteers and staff in the same tool, sometimes sensitive beneficiary data, budgets that rule out licence inflation. Mathieu Haye handles both profiles directly, from scoping to production. Working formats are described on the collaboration page, and the full service range on the AI freelance page.
CRM problems rarely get solved in the abstract: book a scoping call or describe your situation to my AI agent. The e-Enfance / 3018 case is detailed on the projects page and the rest of the work on the home page. What I build

## What changes: one tool, built around your process.

Four project families come up again and again. Every time, the same principle: start from your real process, ship a usable V1, improve after real use.
Most requested

## Marketplace MVP

Supply meets demand with payments built in: sign-up on both sides, profiles, matching, commissions or automatic payouts.
Automated commissions and payouts
Seller and buyer onboarding
Matching, reviews, notifications
Moderation back-office included
Best for
testing a two-sided business with real paying users, before investing further.
Discuss this project

## Vertical B2B SaaS & internal tool

The logic of your process, not a generalist vendor's. Multi-tenant accounts, roles, subscriptions, dashboards.
Multi-organisation accounts and roles
Built-in subscriptions and billing
Import and export of your data
Business dashboards
Best for
SMEs whose process runs on Excel and five badly connected SaaS, or founders of a niche SaaS.
Discuss this project

## Client portal & back-office

Everything your clients or your teams need to see and do, in one place: case tracking, documents, approvals, automatic follow-ups instead of emails and attachments.
Secure client area
Approval workflows
Document generation
Connected to your existing tools
Best for
firms, agencies and service operators who run their clients through email and shared spreadsheets.
Discuss this project

## Community platform

Members, content, events, messaging, moderation: your community on your own turf, with your rules, rather than on a Facebook group or a free Slack.
Profiles, memberships, payments
Content and events
Email and in-app notifications
Moderation and roles

associations, clubs, professional networks, paid communities that want to own their audience.
Discuss this project
Recently shipped, anonymised: a B2B monitoring SaaS designed solo and now in production, community platform work, several business back-offices. The starting point is often the same: spreadsheets and tools that do not talk to each other. The end point: a single workspace, used every day. Your project is not on the list? We scope it in 30 minutes.
How a project works

## A V1 in production, not a six-month project.

We slice the scope down to the version that does the job, ship it to production in 4 to 6 weeks, then improve based on real-world use rather than assumptions.
01

### 30-minute scoping call

We start from your process and the expected outcome, not a 40-page spec. You leave with a V1 perimeter, a timeline and a written quote.
02

### V1 built in 4 to 6 weeks

Main flow, accounts, payments if needed, back-office. You watch the product move forward every week on a staging URL.
03

### Production deployment

Deployment, monitoring, backups. You receive the handoff documentation and admin access.
04

### Iterate after real use

Real users decide what is missing. We prioritise and ship in small increments, through a monthly retainer if you want a steady rhythm.
Framework & trust

## Your product, your keys.

A custom application is only useful if it holds up over time. Here is the framework, the same for every project.
01

### You own the code

A Git repository you own, admin access, clear documentation. Everything is transferable to another team whenever you decide, with no hidden lock.
02

### GDPR & France / EU hosting

Data exportable at any time, compliance considered from the scoping call onwards.
03

### One single point of contact

The person who scopes is the one who codes, deploys and maintains. No subcontracting. NDA signed on request before any detailed discussion.
04

### Serious about production

Deployment with monitoring and backups, handoff doc delivered with the product. Maintenance and evolutions through a monthly retainer, at your pace.
FAQ

## Questions I get about these projects.

How much does an MVP cost?
The price depends on scope, framed in a 30-minute call. The public anchors: a Sprint from 400 EUR for a small, well-defined scope; a full 4-to-6-week MVP on quote; a monthly retainer on quote for evolutions. The quote is written before anything starts.
How fast is my application delivered?
A genuinely usable V1 goes to production in 4 to 6 weeks in most cases: main flow, user accounts, payments if needed, back-office. A small, well-defined scope fits in a 1-week Sprint. You watch progress every week on a staging URL.
Who owns the code?
Fully yours: a Git repository in your name, admin access, handoff documentation. Everything is transferable to another team at any time, with no hidden dependencies and no proprietary licence.
What tools do you use, and why?
Proven, well-documented building blocks: Next.js for the application, Supabase for data and authentication, Stripe for payments. Standard choices that any developer can pick up. I also rely on modern tooling to deliver faster.
What if I already have developers?
No problem: I join as an additional developer on a well-defined scope, in your repository or a dedicated one, with code reviews and clear documentation. Your team takes over without friction; that handoff is planned from day one.
What happens after launch?
The application ships to production with monitoring and backups, and you receive handoff documentation. Then two options: your team takes over, or a monthly retainer covers maintenance and evolutions.

## Shall we scope your V1?

Describe the product or tool you have in mind. In 30 minutes, I tell you what a V1 contains, how fast it ships, and at what price.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email
Mathieu Haye, a freelance developer specialised in AI and automation based in Paris, designs and ships custom web applications and MVPs to production in 4 to 6 weeks: marketplaces, vertical B2B SaaS, internal business tools for SMEs, client portals, back-offices and community platforms. The approach starts from your processes: a genuinely usable V1 first, then improvements decided on real-world use.

## Custom app or off-the-shelf SaaS?

If an off-the-shelf SaaS covers your need for a reasonable subscription, buy it: custom development is not justified. It becomes rational when several subscriptions, exports and double data entry stand in for a process. A custom business application encodes your exact process and replaces that pile. A middle case: if your current tools are fine but do not talk to each other, an n8n automation is often enough, with no app to build. The 30-minute scoping call exists to pick the right option, including when the right answer is to build nothing. Formats and price anchors are detailed on the collaboration page; delivered work is shown on the projects page.

## What if the need goes beyond the app?

Looking for a CRM shaped around your sales cycle? That is a neighbouring but distinct job, told on the custom CRM page. If your product needs an embedded AI agent (customer answers, document search), the AI agent developer page covers those cases; for the full picture of services, see the AI freelance page.
Have a product or a tool in mind? Describe it to my AI agent or go through the collaboration page to book a scoping call. See also: the AI agent for SMEs, your AI maturity, AI visibility (GEO), the homepage and the journal on applied AI.

## /en/n8n-automation

**Titre :** n8n expert freelance · Workflow automation · Mathieu Haye
**Description :** Repetitive tasks disappear, not your team. Freelance n8n consultant: process automation, 30-minute scoping, documented workflows.

n8n expert freelance

# n8n automation: repetitive tasks disappear. Not your team.

Your tools talk to each other and your repetitive tasks run on their own, day and night. Not everything is worth automating: we start with the process that costs you the most.
What to automate

## What you do by hand runs on its own.

Each workflow replaces a chain of copy-paste between tools. Here are examples, drawn from real projects.

## B2B prospecting that feeds your sales team

Before: your salespeople spent their mornings hunting for contacts and re-typing them into the CRM. After: collection, qualification and the first outreach happen on their own, overnight. The team wakes up to qualified prospects.
From contact collection to the personalized email
Deduplication and contact qualification
Scheduled follow-ups, nothing forgotten
Zero manual data entry
Outcome
a prospect pool fed continuously; your salespeople talk to customers instead of hunting for them.

## Press monitoring that filters for you

A monitoring workflow that watches sources and feeds, filters the noise, and automatically flags only what concerns you, summarized and sorted, in your usual channel. No more Google alerts buried in off-topic noise.
Continuous multi-source monitoring
Automatic filtering and summarizing
Alerts only on what matters
Delivered to Slack, email or Notion
Outcome
you never miss anything relevant, without spending an hour a day reading feeds.

## Newsletter and content, no human in the loop

A content pipeline that collects, formats, schedules and distributes, with no human in the loop. The newsletter goes out on its own, content publishes at the right time, and you only touch the process to approve if you want to.
Automatic collection and formatting
Scheduling and multi-channel distribution
Optional human approval step
Traceability on every send
Outcome
a steady presence held with no effort, a publishing routine that no longer depends on your availability.

## CRM sync, notifications, payments

Before: the same information was re-typed into two or three tools, with discrepancies along the way. After: the CRM updates itself, the team is notified at the right moment, payment reminders go out without fail.
Sync between CRM and third-party tools
Targeted internal notifications
Payment reminders and reconciliation
Automatic consolidated reporting
Outcome
an end to double data entry and data that drifts from one tool to another.
Yours gets framed in 30 minutes, once your process is described: goal, tools involved, expected time saved.
Why n8n, how we build

## From described process to workflow in production.

n8n is an open-source automation tool: your workflows stay readable and editable. Every build follows the same four steps.
01

### Process audit

We identify the repetitive task, the tools to connect and the error cases. We measure what it costs today in manual time. If automation is not worth its cost, I tell you at this stage.
02

### Workflow build

I build, test and harden the workflow: explicit error handling, retries and alerts. Nothing breaks in silence, and you validate the result on real cases before going live.
03

### Handoff and ownership

You get the exported workflows, the access and the documentation, with a hands-on walkthrough for your team.
04

### Maintenance as needed

Monthly support on quote: monitoring, fixes and adaptations when a third-party tool changes. You always know who to call.
Trust & terms

## The terms, before we start.

Automating a process means trusting it with part of your business. You know what you own, where your data lives and who to call.
•

### Workflow ownership

Your workflows belong to you: exports, admin access, documentation.
•

### Your data in France

Hosting in France is available: your data stays with you and GDPR compliance is easier.
•

### Single point of contact

One person scopes, builds, deploys and maintains. No subcontracting.
•

### Maintenance and NDA

Monthly support on quote for long-term follow-up. NDA signed on request, before any detailed discussion about your processes.
FAQ

## Questions about n8n automation.

What can you automate with n8n?
Any repetitive process that connects several tools: CRM sync, email follow-up, prospecting, monitoring and alerts, content distribution, payment reminders, reporting. If a task means copying data from one tool to another by hand, it can almost always be automated. The scoping call is there to spot the task with the best return on your time.
Self-hosted n8n or cloud?
Both are possible. Self-hosting keeps your data on your own server; the cloud is faster to set up. The choice is made during scoping, based on your data sensitivity and your volume.
Do I keep my workflows?
Yes. You get the exported files, admin access and the documentation. You can take back control or hand the workflows to another team at any time.
What if a workflow breaks?
Every workflow ships with explicit error handling: retries, logs and alerts on failure, so you are warned before your customers are. A monthly support plan, on quote, covers monitoring, fixes and adaptations when a third-party tool changes.
How much does an automation cost?
Scope is framed in 30 minutes. A first, focused need often fits in a Sprint from €400; a full pipeline is priced on quote, depending on the tools to connect and the error cases to cover. Monthly support is also on quote. Everything is set before we start, with no surprises along the way.
Do I need a technical team on my side?
No. The workflow is designed, tested, deployed and documented end to end, from scoping to production. You do not need an in-house developer. If you already have a technical team, I come in as extra support on the automation side.
Is this different from an AI agent?
Yes. This page is about process automation and making your tools talk to each other. For an assistant that converses with your teams or customers, see the AI agent developer page.

## A process to automate?

Describe the repetitive task eating your time. In 30 minutes, I will tell you whether a workflow replaces it, how long it takes and what budget range to expect.
Book a call Chat with my AI agent Direct email

Portfolio

# Turns your processes into simple, reliable software, from Paris.

Every project here started with the same question: did it really need to be built? The ones below passed that filter. The result: less manual entry, fewer errors, tools that hold up day after day. Everything is in production or tested in real conditions.
Projects

## Projects shipped, results you can verify.

01. Custom CRM build

## e-Enfance / 3018, Emma custom CRM

Custom CRMUnified multichannel queueDistress-signal detectionRealtime supervision
Client
e-Enfance / 3018
Scope
Emma, all-in-one custom CRM
Integrations
Chat, phone, email, WhatsApp, Messenger
Role
Freelance consultant

### The starting point

3018 is the French national hotline for protecting minors online, run by the e-Enfance association. I first worked on their existing market CRM. Day to day, listeners juggled several channels and scattered information, and the tool reached its limits when cases turned urgent.

### What changed

Today, listeners work on Emma, a single workstation designed for them. Every channel (chat, phone, email, WhatsApp, Messenger) lands in one queue. Distress signals are detected automatically and suicide-risk cases rise to the top. Each case is documented in a structured, exportable file, and supervisors follow activity in real time. Everything is hosted in France, on sensitive data, with a full audit trail.

### What it proves

Designing and shipping a complete operations platform end to end, on sensitive data. Not just configuring an existing tool.
02. AI product

## IA Brew. AI Newsletter

n8n20+ sourcesAutomated weekly send
Type
Automated newsletter
Sources
20+ feeds tracked
Frequency
Weekly
Human input
None, from collection to send

### The starting point

Keeping up with AI news took hours of reading every week: dozens of sources, plenty of duplicates, little usable signal.

### What changed

The newsletter now writes itself. More than 20 sources are collected, duplicates merged, the best stories selected, summarised and laid out, and the email ships every week. Nobody touches anything, and every step is logged so any anomaly can be traced.

### What it proves

The same pattern applies to any team monitoring workflow: collect, sort, summarise, distribute. The difference between a gadget and a reliable tool is tracking every step.
03. Data pipeline + GenAI

## Multi-source Job Scorer + ATS PDF Generator

3 job platforms240+ offers analysedATS-ready PDFs
Sources
WTTJ, JobTeaser, LinkedIn
Jobs scored
240+
Auto-generated
ATS CV + cover letter in PDF
Scoring
Weighted to the profile

### The starting point

Serious job hunting took hours: sorting offers from three platforms, then tailoring a CV and cover letter for each role. And most CVs are first read by tracking software (ATS) before any human sees them: an overly creative layout sinks an application without anyone noticing.

### What changed

The tool pulls offers from the three platforms, scores them on weighted criteria (240 offers analysed so far) and presents everything in a filterable table. For each promising offer, it automatically generates a CV and cover letter tailored to the role, exported as PDFs built to be parsed correctly by tracking software. The sorting that took hours now runs itself; only the decision remains.

### What it proves

Data processing is only worth its rigor: if the data is badly structured, the score means nothing. The same standard applies to your business data.
04. Desktop security app

## Juice Jacking Guard. USB Threat Monitor (Windows)

Windows 10 / 11USB securityBlocked on insertionLocal, no telemetry
Platform
Windows 10 / 11
Threats covered
BadUSB, Rubber Ducky, O.MG Cable, juice jacking, USB payloads
Size
~4,900 lines, 20 modules, 11 subsystems
Distribution
Single-file .exe installer (23.3 MB)

### The starting point

A USB device is never harmless. Windows trusts whatever it declares: a fake stick posing as a keyboard can type 200 commands in seconds, a rigged cable can steal data. No antivirus catches it in time.

### What changed

With Juice Jacking Guard installed, any unknown device is blocked before it can even speak to Windows, then classified across four risk levels. A new keyboard has to prove a human is behind it: a specific key to press that an automated payload can neither read nor guess. Storage devices are scanned read-only on insertion, with an optional reputation check. Everything stays local, with no telemetry.

### Download (Windows 10 / 11)

Download .zip (recommended, 22.7 MB) Direct .exe (23.3 MB)
SHA-256 (.zip): e37364564f1eed9af699b85a40dae6e5fdbf9f25efba72f3002f6d0e0b41ffac
SHA-256 (.exe): 1132af4c3a2158f17806a7720b0a46d4c477799bae78b6a81d3a315cfa30812a
The installer is unsigned, so Chrome / Edge and Windows SmartScreen will warn before download and at first launch. The .zip is recommended: browsers trust archives more than raw executables. After extraction, if Windows shows "Windows protected your PC", click More info → Run anyway and verify the SHA-256 above. The app is local, with no telemetry, and only reaches VirusTotal if you enable the optional check.

### What it proves

Taking a niche, half-understood threat and shipping a defence that works, down to the installer and a clean uninstall.
05. Multi-asset tracker

## Bloomberg-Style Dashboard

6 assets tracked3 daily briefsTelegram alerts
Scope
6 assets (crypto, stocks, ETF)
Briefs
3 per day, automatic
Alerts
Telegram, 3 tiers
Schedule
08:50 / 13:00 / 17:30 Paris

### The starting point

Following six assets seriously meant a wall of open tabs, indicators to recompute and decisions made in the heat of the moment.

### What changed

A single dashboard, one I use every morning, follows BTC, ETH, SOL, NVDA, TTE and CW8. It pulls prices, computes the technicals and pushes three briefs a day to Telegram, at the hours that matter: before the open, at midday, after the close. On top of that: a scenario simulator, a recurring-investment tracker, a market events calendar and a trade journal.

### What it proves

Data collection, computation, automated briefs, scheduling: the same foundation as any business dashboard. Only the subject differs.
06. Pine Script + backtest

## IchimokuSignal Pro

TradingViewSingle GO / WAIT signalBacktest with real costs
Type
Indicator + backtester
Version
v3.3 (Bounce + 15% SL)
Net return (AAOI, 5y)
+187% vs +1088% B&H
Win rate / PF / MaxDD
54% / 1.62 / 24%

### The starting point

Most Ichimoku indicators fire too often on long-term charts. I wanted a single GO or WAIT signal for stock picking, validated by a backtest that includes commissions and execution costs.

### What changed

The indicator combines ten criteria into one composite score, with a 15% protective stop and safeguards against false signals. A backtester replays the logic candle by candle, round-trip fees and slippage included.

### Results (AAOI, 5y)

Strategy: +187%, 54% win rate, max drawdown -24%.
Buy-and-hold baseline: +1088%, max drawdown -62%.
So the strategy loses on absolute return, but cuts the downside risk by more than half.

### What it proves

A single-stock backtest is always partly overfit; choosing a strategy is choosing a risk profile.
07. Finance modeling

## Real Estate Investment Model

INSEE data34 cities rankedTax regime comparator
Scope
SCI/IS vs LMNP, mortgage
Data
INSEE, notary stats, rental yields
Cities ranked
34 French metros
Purpose
Size my own portfolio

### The starting point

Choosing between an SCI at corporate tax and LMNP is too often done on gut feeling, even though the tax wrapper weighs more than the purchase price on the final return.

### What changed

A model now compares both regimes for every deal: year-by-year cash flow, 10-year IRR, tax impact, net equity at exit. It includes mortgage scenarios, an index ranking 34 French metros by rental attractiveness, and macro scenarios (rates, inflation). I use it on deals I underwrite.

### What it proves

Turning a complex, numbers-heavy decision into a tool that settles it. The same reasoning applies to any structured investment decision.
08. Quant trading

## Crypto Trading Bot (MEXC)

Automated tradingPublished results, wins and lossesBuilt-in kill switch
Strategy
Mean reversion, BTC + ETH
Paper P&L
+3.2% over 90 days
Live P&L
-2.1% over 30 days
Win rate
46% (214 trades)

### The starting point

Short-term crypto pairs often overshoot their equilibrium. The hypothesis: when two indicators flag an excess at the same time, a snap-back is more likely than a continuation.

### What changed

A bot tests that hypothesis in real conditions: a double-confirmation entry filter, capped position sizes, automatic exits, a live results curve and a kill switch. First in simulation, then with a small-scale live pilot.

### Results

Simulation (90 days): +3.2%.
Live pilot (30 days, 10% of simulated size): -2.1%, 46% win rate.
The gap comes from fees and execution costs the simulation did not model; fees alone explain roughly 4 points.

### What it proves

I also publish what loses. A simulation that ignores friction costs overstates the result; the corrected version is still running, in simulation, until proven otherwise.
Technical note: depending on the project, this work relies on TypeScript, Python, n8n and Claude models.

## /blog/2026-07-15-donnees-pretes-ia-fondation-agents

**Titre :** Agents IA en entreprise : la donnée n'est pas prête · Blog Mathieu Haye
**Description :** Le 14 juillet 2026, Xebia lance Axis, une fondation data agentique. Le vrai frein des agents IA en entreprise n'est pas le modèle, c'est la donnée.

Tous les articles Data & Analytics · 15 juillet 2026 · 6 min de lecture

# Agents IA en entreprise : la donnée n'est pas prête

Le 14 juillet 2026, la société de services data Xebia a lancé Axis, une fondation data agentique. Derrière le produit, un signal qui vaut pour toute PME : le vrai frein des agents IA en entreprise n'est pas le modèle, c'est une donnée qui n'est pas prête à ce qu'une machine agisse dessus.
L'essentiel en 30 secondes
Le 14 juillet 2026, la société de services data Xebia a lancé Axis, une fondation data agentique qui combine des agents IA propriétaires et des ingénieurs data humains pour préparer les données d'entreprise à la production d'IA.
Xebia annonce des migrations environ 3 fois plus rapides qu'une équipe purement humaine, un effet de levier des agents estimé à 10 fois, et des évaluations initiales bouclées en 2 à 4 semaines au lieu de plusieurs mois.
Le vrai goulot d'étranglement de l'IA en entreprise n'est pas le modèle : selon Gartner, 60 % des projets d'IA non soutenus par des données prêtes pour l'IA seront abandonnés d'ici fin 2026.
Un agent qui agit sur des données incohérentes ou non auditables n'est pas un gain de productivité, c'est un risque de production. La donnée gouvernée est le prérequis, pas l'option.
Pendant deux ans, la course à l'IA d'entreprise s'est jouée sur les modèles. Le lancement du 14 juillet 2026 déplace le projecteur là où le travail se cache vraiment : la couche data. Xebia, une société de services data et IA, met sur le marché un produit dont le nom même trahit le problème du secteur, préparer les données pour que des agents puissent enfin agir dessus sans casser quoi que ce soit.

## Ce que Xebia a annoncé le 14 juillet

Le 14 juillet 2026, Xebia a rendu disponible Axis, présenté comme une fondation data agentique (GlobeNewswire). Le constat de départ est simple : la plupart des entreprises ont déjà migré leurs données vers le cloud, mais restent incapables de déployer de l'IA en production parce que leur socle data et leur gouvernance ne sont pas prêts pour des agents autonomes, qui exigent des données consistantes et auditables.
Axis couvre le cycle de vie complet de la donnée à travers six modules : Readiness, Platform, Knowledge, Migration, Observability et Operations. Le modèle de travail est hybride : des équipes humaines fixent la stratégie et contrôlent la qualité, pendant que des agents IA propriétaires exécutent l'évaluation, la migration, la surveillance et la remédiation. Xebia avance des chiffres précis : des migrations environ 3 fois plus rapides qu'une approche humaine classique, un effet de levier des agents de l'ordre de 10 fois, une réduction des coûts d'infrastructure de 30 à 50 % en deux ans, et des évaluations initiales livrées en 2 à 4 semaines là où un chantier traditionnel s'étale sur 12 à 18 mois. "Axis comble cet écart, pour que nos clients passent à l'entreprise agentique plus vite que leurs concurrents", résume Anand Sahay, PDG de Xebia.

## Pourquoi la donnée, pas le modèle, est le vrai goulot ?

La donnée est le vrai goulot parce qu'un agent autonome ne se contente pas de lire un tableau de bord : il agit. Un modèle de langage peut halluciner une phrase sans grande conséquence ; un agent branché sur des données incohérentes déclenche une mauvaise action, à grande échelle et sans supervision. Le chiffre qui cadre le mieux le problème vient de Gartner : d'ici fin 2026, les organisations abandonneront 60 % des projets d'IA non soutenus par des données prêtes pour l'IA (Gartner). Le même cabinet a constaté, dans une enquête auprès de 248 responsables de la gestion des données, que 63 % des organisations n'ont pas, ou ne savent pas si elles ont, les bonnes pratiques de gestion de données nécessaires à l'IA.
La nuance tient dans l'expression "prête pour l'IA". Une donnée prête pour un rapport mensuel doit être exacte à une date donnée. Une donnée prête pour un agent doit être exacte en continu, documentée, traçable et gouvernée, parce que l'agent la consulte des milliers de fois par jour et prend des décisions sans qu'un humain relise chaque requête. C'est un standard beaucoup plus exigeant, et c'est précisément celui que la majorité des entreprises n'atteignent pas. Gartner prévoit d'ailleurs que plus de 40 % des projets d'IA agentique seront abandonnés d'ici fin 2027, en citant des coûts qui dérapent, une valeur floue et des contrôles de risque insuffisants.

## L'économie de l'ingénierie data agentique

Le pari d'Axis, et de produits comparables, c'est d'appliquer les agents non pas à la couche visible, l'assistant qui répond au client, mais à la couche invisible, le travail d'ingénierie data qui rend cette réponse fiable. L'effet de levier de 10 fois annoncé par Xebia ne dit pas qu'un agent remplace dix ingénieurs ; il dit qu'un ingénieur outillé d'agents traite dix fois plus de tables, de schémas et de règles de qualité qu'à la main. La migration, la cartographie de schémas, la détection d'anomalies sont des tâches répétitives, volumineuses et vérifiables, exactement le terrain où un agent supervisé est le plus rentable. C'est la suite logique de la couche sémantique qui traduit les données pour les agents : après avoir décrit le sens des données, il faut encore les mettre en ordre.
Reste que le chiffre à surveiller n'est pas la vitesse, c'est la gouvernance. Accélérer une migration de 3 fois n'a aucun intérêt si l'on industrialise au passage des données fausses. C'est pourquoi le modèle hybride compte plus que les agents eux-mêmes : "les ingénieurs humains travaillent aux côtés des agents pour construire et exploiter des plateformes data de production, avec la gouvernance intégrée à la plateforme elle-même", précise Niels Zeilemaker, directeur technique mondial Data et IA de Xebia. Le contexte de marché confirme l'urgence : selon une étude d'OutSystems publiée en juillet 2026, 96 % des entreprises utilisent désormais des agents IA, mais 94 % s'inquiètent d'une prolifération non maîtrisée (OutSystems). Les agents arrivent plus vite que les fondations censées les porter.

## Ce que ça change pour une PME, et pour mon quotidien

Pour une PME, la leçon est libératrice : la partie coûteuse d'un projet IA n'est pas le modèle, qui se loue à l'API, mais la mise en ordre des données. Une entreprise qui veut un agent commercial ou un tableau de bord fiable a d'abord besoin d'un socle : des données consistantes, nommées de la même façon partout, mises à jour proprement. C'est un chantier moins spectaculaire qu'un agent conversationnel, mais c'est lui qui décide si le reste tient en production.
C'est exactement l'ordre de priorité que je suis en mission. Sur le tableau de bord Bloomberg que j'ai monté avec Claude Haiku 4.5 pour suivre un portefeuille, l'essentiel du travail n'était pas le modèle mais la structuration des données de marché pour qu'elles soient interrogeables sans erreur. Sur les dashboards KPI construits pour Profile Club avec Apps Script, ou sur les 93 nœuds n8n qui alimentent la newsletter d'IA Brew, la règle est la même : la plus grosse part de l'effort part dans le nettoyage, la normalisation et la fiabilisation des flux, et une part bien plus faible dans la couche intelligente qui s'appuie dessus. Un agent posé sur des données bancales ne fait qu'automatiser l'erreur plus vite.
La vraie question de 2026 n'est donc pas "quel modèle choisir", mais "mes données sont-elles prêtes à ce qu'une machine agisse dessus sans moi ?". Tant que la réponse est non, l'agent le plus avancé restera une démo.

## Questions fréquentes

### Qu'est-ce qu'une donnée prête pour l'IA ?

Une donnée prête pour l'IA est consistante, documentée, traçable et gouvernée en continu, pas seulement exacte à un instant donné. Un agent autonome la consulte des milliers de fois par jour et agit sans relecture humaine systématique, ce qui exige un niveau de qualité et de gouvernance supérieur à celui d'un simple rapport. Selon Gartner, seule une minorité d'entreprises atteint ce standard aujourd'hui.

### Qu'a annoncé Xebia le 14 juillet 2026 ?

Xebia a lancé Axis, une fondation data agentique qui combine des agents IA propriétaires et des ingénieurs data humains à travers six modules, de l'évaluation à l'exploitation. La société annonce des migrations environ 3 fois plus rapides, un effet de levier des agents de l'ordre de 10 fois et une réduction des coûts d'infrastructure de 30 à 50 % en deux ans.

### Pourquoi tant de projets d'IA échouent-ils ?

Parce que le blocage vient rarement du modèle et souvent des données. Gartner prévoit que 60 % des projets d'IA non soutenus par des données prêtes pour l'IA seront abandonnés d'ici fin 2026, et que plus de 40 % des projets d'IA agentique le seront d'ici fin 2027. Sans socle data gouverné, un agent amplifie les incohérences au lieu de créer de la valeur.
Mathieu Haye.
22 ans, freelance builder en CRM, Data et IA appliquée. Apps IA, automatisations et CRM sur-mesure pour PME, startups et associations.
